title: tillandsia monadelpha
intro: Tillandsia monadelpha, also known as the 'Lance-leaved Air Plant', is a species of epiphyte, a type of plant that grows on other surfaces without deriving nutrients from them. Traditionally, Tillandsia monadelpha has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant also include its potential to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. In herbal preparations, Tillandsia monadelpha is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
